channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Survivors of Myanmar’s Landmine Blasts Struggle With Severed Limbs and Shattered Lives
item.description: The pain from their injuries hasn’t gone away. Meanwhile, they face challenges in earning a livelihood.

item.title: Why Anthropic Is Fighting With Trump (Again)
item.description: The AI company is back in the U.S. government’s crosshairs.

item.title: What Will China Learn From the Iran War?
item.description: As the conflict winds down, Beijing is taking notes.

item.title: Trump Puts Russia-Ukraine War on the Back Burner
item.description: A lackluster attitude highlights divisions between the United States and its G-7 allies.

item.title: Albania’s flamingo protests target Donald Trump’s son-in-law
item.description: Jared Kushner’s beach development brings trouble for a prime minister

item.title: Deal or no deal, oil prices will stay high for months
item.description: The pre-war days of $60 crude are not coming back soon

item.title: ‘It’s Not Competitive in Nature’
item.description: India’s ambassador to Washington insists “Make in India” doesn’t conflict with “America First.”

item.title: Taiwan Bet Too Big on Washington
item.description: Trump’s willingness to sell out allies should cause a rethink in Taipei.

item.title: Iran Is a Bigger Defeat Than Vietnam
item.description: A war of choice has turned into a strategic disaster for Washington.

item.title: Trump’s Drug War Tactics Don’t Work
item.description: Development aid remains the best way to reduce drug production.

item.title: Asian allies are doomed to hug Donald Trump close
item.description: Forget middle-power coalitions. Japan and neighbours know that only America can deter China

item.title: America’s bull market has entered its manic phase
item.description: Options markets show optimism is giving way to euphoria

item.title: America’s bull market has entered its manic phase
item.description: Options markets show optimism is giving way to euphoria

item.title: The coming El Niño could be the strongest ever recorded
item.description: It is bad news for a warming world

item.title: Scammers are preying on America’s illegal immigrants
item.description: Fake lawyers see opportunity in Donald Trump’s crackdown

item.title: It’s Time for the United States to Leave the Middle East
item.description: The Iran war is only the latest in a string of U.S. failures.

item.title: KMT Chair Returns From Her US Tour
item.description: Cheng Li-wun's two-week trip to the U.S. didn't see the high-profile White House meetings she sought, but still served as a signal of her political ambitions.

item.title: Did AI write this article?
item.description: We track how artificial intelligence is changing five fields

item.title: The Fed Has Been Honest and Stupid
item.description: It would be easy—and a mistake—to blame the US Federal Reserve’s many blunders on corrupt bureaucrats or imagined conspiracies. The truth of the matter is that the central bank relies on an outdated regional map and lagging indicators that no longer tell the whole story, while battling macroeconomic blazes that politicians stoke.

item.title: Travel Brazil’s mirror-state to see the country’s future
item.description: No one has ever been elected president in Brazil without carrying Minas Gerais

item.title: A New Framework for Climate Displacement
item.description: Global warming is already uprooting communities and impoverishing people who remain in vulnerable areas—a crisis that is often overshadowed by the focus on decarbonization. But the Global Climate Mobility Principles offer policymakers a framework for ensuring that these individuals maintain their agency and rights.

item.title: Mongolia and Africa: Time to Reignite a Forgotten Partnership
item.description: During the Cold War, Mongolia forged surprisingly warm relationships with African countries. It’s time to resume those ties.

item.title: Japan Eyes a Homegrown FMS System as Defense Exports Become a Strategic Tool
item.description: The government is considering establishing a new organization – to manage a Japanese-style Foreign Military Sales system and promote defense exports.

item.title: Peace With Iran Is All About Lebanon Now
item.description: US President Donald Trump says that he "calls the shots" on negotiating with Iran, and Israel has no choice but to submit to a deal that undermines its security interests. But a deal that makes Trump the protector of Lebanon—and, by extension, Iran-backed Hezbollah—could put the US and Israel on a collision course.

item.title: It’s Time to Stop Hunting Monsters
item.description: Foreign intervention is a policy with a long history of disaster.

item.title: South Korea’s Students Speak Out About the Local Election Ballot Shortage
item.description: South Korean university students framed the issue as one of democratic accountability, while avoiding getting caught in the partisan fight that has ensued.

item.title: China’s Limited Advance in El Salvador
item.description: In the context of El Salvador’s transformation under Bukele, its relationship with China continues to be both limited and contradictory.

item.title: Kazakhstan Admitted to Corruption. Nothing Changed.
item.description: While the state recovers billions in London and The Hague, oil workers in Kazakhstan still receive a fraction of what they are owed.

item.title: Everyone Lost the War With Iran
item.description: Months of fighting revealed that multiple countries can impose costs, but none can impose order.

item.title: Israel Has a Plan to Keep Calling Its Own Shots
item.description: Israel is resisting Donald Trump’s demands that it fall in line with his Middle East policy.

item.title: Every British Prime Minister’s Nightmare
item.description: The true nature of Britain’s “special relationship” with the United States has far more to do with American financiers’ willingness to keep borrowing to purchase British government debt than with history, culture, or Britain’s defense needs. Every UK Prime Minister must bear this burden, which is rapidly becoming unsustainable.

item.title: Why the US Should Back Pakistan in Blacklisting BLA in the UNSC
item.description: The U.S. and Pakistan engage in substantial intelligence-sharing and counterterrorism cooperation. These could be at risk.

item.title: The end of the war in Iran threatens “glorious failure” for Israel
item.description: Donald Trump’s deal with Iran would leave America’s ally without any strategic gains

item.title: India’s Strongest Regional Party Collapses Thanks to Modi’s BJP
item.description: Legislators of the Trinamool Congress, which ruled West Bengal for three straight terms, have left the party in droves, leaving it a mere shadow of its former self.

item.title: Why India Does Not Interest China
item.description: The Chinese see Indians as not having absorbed the lessons that China did from its humiliation at the hands of the West.

item.title: How to Say No to an Imperial President
item.description: Trump’s expansion of executive power would make even Richard Nixon blush.

item.title: NATO’s Permanent Crisis
item.description: The alliance has survived 80 years of disagreement—and it will survive again.

item.title: The Middle East Power Paradox
item.description: How the Iran war will transform America’s military role.

item.title: The Kentucky Colonel Who Drives Australian Foreign Policy
item.description: The country's foreign policy is being distorted by militarized thinking and a misplaced faith in U.S. power.

item.title: A Four-nation Electricity Alliance Could Accelerate ASEAN’s Energy Transition
item.description: Power trading between Vietnam, Thailand, Malaysia, and Singapore could be an important step toward the realization of the ambitious ASEAN Power Grid.

item.title: Myanmar’s Min Aung Hlaing Begins First Visit to China Since Appointment as President
item.description: Whatever the misgivings of the country's security establishment, China is set to remain an important partner of the new military-backed civilian government.

item.title: Asian Synthetic Drug Seizures Surged to Record Highs in 2025, UN Says
item.description: The UNODC warned of a growing convergence between drug trafficking operations and other criminal enterprises, particularly online scamming operations.

item.title: Beyond the Malacca Dilemma: China’s Emerging Corridor-Hedging Logic
item.description: China’s approach to external connectivity recognizes that different trade routes perform different functions under different political and operational conditions.
